code: @Manual{,   title = {statsapps: Interactive 'Shiny' Apps for Building Statistical Intuition},   author = {Vikram B. Baliga},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/vbaliga/statsapps}, }

title: statsapps
text: statsapps R package features interactive Shiny apps help learn concepts data science & statistics. apps run locally use visualization, simulation, repeated sampling illustrate topics probability distributions, permutation tests, analysis variance, linear regression, sums squares. see statsapps package website.

title: statsapps
text: Install current release CRAN: get bug fix use feature development version, can instead install development version statsapps GitHub repo :

title: statsapps
text: run_distributions_app() run_sums_squares_app() run_anova_app() run_linear_reg_app() run_permutation_app() apps open default web browser. Set launch.browser = FALSE disable behavior.

title: statsapps
text: apps can started via functions statsapps. app can started using function begins run_. example, use run_permutation_app() start Shiny app Permutation Test. open new window default web browser Shiny app. example showcase Permutation Test app, demonstrates repeated random reassignment observations can used build null distribution two-sample permutation test. statsapps, app interactive allows see results individual permutations, result permutation incrementally builds null distribution. several tabs within main window app. first two tabs provide explanation original data. third tab shows results performing permutations. buttons left sidebar allow perform permutations visualize outcome recent permutation.

title: statsapps
text: Feedback bugs, improvements, /feature requests welcome. Please see Issues section GitHub make bug fix request feature request.
